content.text: THE HEART OF GOD / CAMPUS REVIVAL / MISSION REVIVAL
- Isaiah 6:8 -
<WORLD MISSION CONGRESS (WMC)> - May 17, Sunday at Kintex 9A Hall
Regarding Transportation for Missionaries upon Arrival

When missionaries arrive, each sending center will be responsible for greeting them at the airport and assisting with their transportation to the World Mission Congress venue or their hotel. It is recommended to use public transportation from Incheon Airport (such as airport buses or trains). Since Incheon Airport is an international airport with excellent public transportation options, using these services will actually be more convenient.
Recruiting Volunteer Interpreters for the Missionary Shepherds’ Conference and K-Mission Camp

We are seeking volunteers who can help with interpretation during the Missionary Shepherds’ Conference and K-Mission Camp. Second-gens or missionaries who can assist with interpretation in English, Spanish, Russian, or other languages are welcome to apply.
For inquiries or to volunteer, please contact Elijah Moon. (010-9988-5527 /
sbsolutemoon@hanmail.net
Group Accommodation for Early Arrivals (May 15–16)

For those arriving early (on May 15–16, before the retreat), we plan to reserve group accommodations. The location will be accessible by airport bus and situated near Ilsan KINTEX. Through a group contract, we aim to provide lodging at a discounted rate.
The estimated cost is 90,000–100,000 KRW per night for a double room.
Further details will be announced later.
Children’s Worship Service During the World Mission Conference (WMC)
During the World Mission Congress (WMC), there will be a Children’s Worship Service for CBF and EBF. The children’s service will be held in the main conference hall located above KINTEX Hall 9A.
While the main sessions are taking place, children will attend their own worship service.
After the service, there will also be Christian animated films and puppet shows prepared especially for the children.
Preparation of the Online Mission Expo Website

We are currently preparing an online website for the Mission Expo, which will also be linked to the main registration site. In addition, an on-site exhibition booth will be set up at the Jongno UBF Headquarters Center.
We plan to begin operating the online expo even before the retreat and will also install and operate a booth at Alpensia in Pyeongchang during the event.
*The World Mission Congress official website opened! Click below image link and go to the official website!
2. <MISSIONARY SHEPHERD CONFERENCE (MSC)> - May 17-20 at Alpensia Resort
Venue: Pyeongchang Alpensia Resort
The main sessions will be held in the Convention Hall, and the worship services will take place in the Concert Hall.
Most accommodations will be double rooms (2 persons per room), and family rooms will also be available for those attending with families.
(If you would like to request a family room, please make sure to indicate it separately on the registration site.)
It takes approximately three hours from KINTEX to Pyeongchang Alpensia, and transportation will be provided.
3. <K-MISSION CAMP>
The K-Mission Camp is a special program held during the Missionary Shepherds Conference (MSC) for mainly second-gens and native attendees who may find it difficult to attend the main conference.

The camp will include cultural and sightseeing activities around Gyeonggi-do and Seoul, such as visits to Everland, the Korean Folk Village, the Han River, Yanghwajin, and Gyeongbokgung Palace. In the evenings, there will be Bible messages, praise, and fellowship prepared especially for the participants.
The camp will take place in Yongin, Gyeonggi-do.
(When applying for accommodations, please make sure to register separately for the K-Mission Camp team, as participants will stay apart from their parents and mentors.)

The K-Mission Camp team will move from KINTEX to the retreat center in Gyeonggi-do on May 17, and after completing the camp, they will travel to Pyeongchang Alpensia by noon on May 20 to join their designated mission trip teams. Those not participating in mission trips will return to the Jongno UBF Center in Seoul instead.
4. <Post-Conference Mission Tours>
Tour-1: Gwangju UBF Historical Tour (2 nights, 3 days)

Itinerary: Visit Daein-dong UBF History Museum in Gwangju, Suncheon National Garden and Suncheon Bay, Aeyangwon (Lepers’ Hospital), Suncheon Christian Museum, Odongdo Island and its Marine Cable Car, and Jeonju Hanok Village.
Team Leader: Chung-Shin Han
Tour-2: Gangwon-do Tour (2 nights, 3 days)
Itinerary: Visit the Sheep Ranch, Goseong DMZ (Panmunjom), Seoraksan Cable Car, Biseondae, and the Rocking Rock.
Team Leader: Joseph Cho
*Both the Gwangju and Gangwon-do teams will depart from Pyeongchang Alpensia at 1:00 PM on May 20, and their tour schedules will begin on the same day.
Tour-3: Jeju Island Tour (2 nights, 3 days)
Itinerary: Seongsan Ilchulbong (Sunrise Peak), Manjanggul Cave, Horseback Riding, Jungmun Cheonjiyeon Waterfall, and more.
Team Leader: Stephen Kim
The Jeju tour team will depart from Pyeongchang Alpensia at 1:00 PM on May 20 and is scheduled to arrive at Gimpo Airport before 4:00 PM by chartered bus.
Flight from Gimpo to Jeju:
Please book your own flight departing between 5:00 PM and 6:30 PM from Gimpo to Jeju.
Our team will provide pickup service at Jeju Airport between 6:30 PM and 8:00 PM.
(Pickup location: Jeju Airport parking lot. The exact meeting point will be announced separately.)
Flight from Jeju to Gimpo (return):
After the Jeju tour ends, we will drop you off at Jeju Airport at 6:00 PM on May 22.
Please reserve your return flight to Gimpo or Gwangju after 7:00 PM.
If you wish to continue traveling in Jeju after May 22 at 6:00 PM, you are welcome to do so at your own discretion.
*Please note that each participant is responsible for booking their own flight tickets (you may use your personal mileage points).
A round-trip ticket between Gimpo and Jeju on Jeju Air is approximately 120,000 KRW.
